💡 Help

Imports & Dependencies

Comma-separated list of npm packages (e.g., axios, lodash, moment)

External library URLs (one per line)

Import statements for your tool implementation

Custom import map for module resolution

Generated Import Configuration

Configure imports above to see preview

Parameters

Test Input

Valid JSON

Execution Console

Console ready. Click "Run Test" to execute.

Test Result

Click "Run Test" to execute the tool with test input

JSON Schema

Auto-generated schema based on your parameter definitions 

Define parameters to see schema

Usage Example

// Call the tool in your task
const result = await __callHostTool__('my-tool', {
  // parameters here
});

console.log('Result:', result);

Model Context Protocol (MCP)

Preview how this tool appears in MCP clients and LLM integrations

Tool Definition

Loading...

Resource

Loading...

💡 Tool Editor Help

Tool Structure

Tools are functions that accept parameters and return results. Define in Definition tab, extract parameters from code, test with JSON input, and preview MCP schema.

✨ Features

Format Code: Ctrl+Shift+F - Auto-indent your tool implementation
Extract Parameters: Parameters tab → Extract from Code button
JSON Validation: Test tab shows green/red indicators
MCP Preview: See how your tool appears to AI/LLM clients
Run Test: F5 or Ctrl+Enter

Parameters

Use Parameters tab to define inputs. Click "Extract from Code" to auto-detect parameters from your function signature. Each parameter has name, type, description, and required flag.

Imports & Dependencies

Add NPM packages (comma-separated), CDN URLs, or ES module imports. Import Map for advanced module resolution. Validates imports on blur.

Testing

Enter JSON matching your parameters in Test tab. Green border = valid, red border = invalid. Results show in Test Result section.

MCP Integration

Model Context Protocol allows Claude and other LLMs to use your tool. Preview tab shows how your tool will appear in MCP clients with parameter schema.
